codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of log-dedup filter. Core dedup logic correctly collapses 3+ consecutive identical lines with (xN) suffix, preserves runs <3, handles timestamp normalization for ISO/syslog/bracketed formats, and appends an accurate summary line. All 4 validation assertions (VAL-DATA-014 through VAL-DATA-017) are properly covered by 31 tests. ANSI stripping, unicode handling, and large output performance are all addressed. Two non-blocking issues noted: an unused helper function in tests and an overly broad `tail` command matcher.

issues.description: Dead code: `repeat()` helper function is defined but never called anywhere in the test file. All tests use `Array(n).fill(...)` directly instead. Should be removed to keep tests clean.

The `TAIL_CMD` regex `/^tail\b/` matches ANY tail command (e.g., `tail README.md`), not just log files. IMPLEMENTATION.md implies log-specific detection. While dedup on non-log tail output is harmless (non-duplicated lines pass through unchanged), this is broader than intended. Consider tightening to `/^tail\b.*\.(log|logs)\b|^tail\b.*\/log\//` or similar.
Grammar: summary format produces '1 unique lines' (singular count with plural noun) when there's exactly 1 unique line. Minor cosmetic issue but worth noting for polish.
The `/g` flag on regex patterns in `normalizeForComparison()` is unnecessary since all patterns are anchored to `^` and can only match once per `.replace()` call. Harmless but misleading.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The filter registry uses first-match-wins ordering. The log-dedup filter's matches() returns true for 'docker logs' commands, but the docker-logs filter is registered earlier and will always intercept those commands first. This means log-dedup's LOG_CMD regex for 'docker logs' is effectively dead code in production. This ordering dependency isn't documented in AGENTS.md — future workers could unknowingly register filters in an order that shadows existing ones.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/filters/index.ts:73-77 — createDockerLogsFilter() precedes createLogDedupFilter(). Both match 'docker logs' via regex. src/filters/log-dedup.ts:109 — LOG_CMD = /^(docker\s+logs|journalctl)\b/

sharedStateObservations.observation: IMPLEMENTATION.md specifies log-dedup should use content-based detection ('output has > 50 lines with duplicates detected'), but the implementation uses command-based matching instead. This deviation is arguably better (avoids scanning all output), but the divergence from spec isn't documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: IMPLEMENTATION.md line 514: 'Input detection: output has > 50 lines with duplicates detected' vs actual implementation using command regex matching (LOG_CMD, TAIL_CMD, CAT_LOG_CMD)

summary: The log-dedup filter implementation is correct and well-tested. All 4 validation assertions pass: 3+ identical line collapse (VAL-DATA-014), <3 run preservation (VAL-DATA-015), timestamp-based pattern dedup (VAL-DATA-016), and summary line (VAL-DATA-017). 31 tests provide thorough coverage including edge cases (empty input, unicode, ANSI codes, large output). Non-blocking issues include an unused test helper, an overly broad tail matcher, a minor grammar issue in the summary format, and unnecessary regex flags. The filter is properly registered in the registry. Overall: clean, well-structured implementation that meets all requirements.
